summary: Bump ComfyUI v0.27.0 -> v0.28.0 across node_lock.json (core tag+commit, frontend/template pins), system_dependencies.json (LOCAL engine), the mpi-ci build-context lock copy, and the Builder COMFYUI_REF (that half is MPI-183, retarget 0.27.0 -> 0.28.0). Drivers: a node gated at 0.28 that the Krea2 edit/inpaint work needs (it runs on the Krea2 1.2 identity-edit LoRA we already ship - no new weight; user has a video with the details), plus 0.28's more optimized int8/int4 paths and the Turing black-image fix on quantized models. Apply the pin rule first (memory project_comfyui_bump_cadence): research the exact minor every near-term node needs and pin to the HIGHEST floor - one bump per wave. Ships as ONE dev image bundled with MPI-341 (single CI dispatch at 0.17.0-dev, BOTH legs), which also gives MPI-340's dev IMAGE tag path its first real proof. Feature wiring is NOT in this card. Full detail in brief.md.

SCOPE CORRECTED (user checked the video, 2026-07-23): the Krea2 storyboard/inpainting capability is NOT gated on ComfyUI core. It is 'Krea2 Edit (grounded)' (grounding_px + system_prompt) from comfyui-krea2edit - a node we ALREADY ship, pinned at 17af8833 - driving the krea2_identity_edit_v1_2 LoRA and qwen3vl_4b_fp8_scaled CLIP we already ship. If our pin lacks the grounded variant that is a node_lock commit bump ONLY (installRequirements:false = volume-resident = no image rebuild), so CHECK THAT FIRST; it may be reachable today. The 0.28 bump therefore stands on its own merits, and the real v0.28.0 notes (Comfy-Org, no breaking changes stated) rank them: Qwen3-VL tokenizer crash fixes (we ship qwen3vl_4b as both the Krea2 edit CLIP and the image describer - most relevant to us), int8/int4 work that is explicitly TURING/16xx-targeted (a LOCAL-GPU win; Pods run Ada/Blackwell so do not size this on Pod speedups), PID 1.5 model support, a text-model sampling speedup and a VAE dynamic-method AttributeError fix. Feature wiring moved out of this card entirely - it is a node-commit question, not a core-version one.

Scope extended (user): added a FINAL STAGE section to brief.md - 342 owns the only Pod rebuild, so it must also close the verification of MPI-341 (all four checks: smoke layer passes on a clean dev build; unpin kornia and confirm the build FAILS at the smoke layer, then restore; +cu130 trio plus /opt/constraints.txt present in the final image; one LTX gen on the dev Pod) and MPI-340's ONE build-gated leg (dev image tag path: move POD_IMAGE_VERSION_DEV and POD_IMAGE_VERSION_CPU_DEV to the real 0.17.0-dev tags, restart, prove a source run pulls the -dev tag while a released run still resolves v0.16.0). Recorded explicitly that MPI-340's other two open items - the fake-BUILD_HASH released-resolves-stable proof and one real promote - are NOT build-gated and must not be parked behind this build.
Derived 5 phase items from plan.md: pin edits, CI dev build, MPI-341 verification, MPI-340 build-gated leg, workflow sweep.

Phase 1 (pin edits) done: node_lock core v0.28.0 / 700821e1364eaab0e8f21c538a2131719fec57bf, frontend 1.45.21 + templates 0.11.9, system_dependencies engine.version 0.28.0, mpi-ci pod node_lock re-synced byte-identical (also closed a stale ComfyUI-MpiNodes pin: ba9e1569 -> aaa1d2d9), builder Dockerfile COMFYUI_REF -> v0.28.0 (MPI-183 retarget).

Research settled in validation.md: 0.28.0 is the newest ComfyUI release so it IS the pin-rule ceiling; all four Windows portable assets exist at the tag so the LOCAL engine URL resolves; comfyui-krea2edit does NOT need a bump for 0.28 because the ref_latents wrapper change landed in ComfyUI c9602625 dated 2026-07-19 which is AFTER the 0.28.0 tag - but that same change WILL break Krea2EditModelPatch on 0.29+ unless the node moves past dc7940f4; the grounded node Krea2EditGroundedEncode is a node-commit question (volume-resident, no image), still out of scope.

Phase 2 done: dev image built and pushed on ComfyUI v0.28.0. v0.17.0-dev-cu130 on Docker Hub (sha256:80351c10), v0.17.0-dev-cpu on GHCR. Both public, step 5a pull-verify passes. MPI-341's node-import smoke layer printed 'node-import smoke test OK' on the clean 0.28 build = MPI-341 check 1 PASSES.

First cu130 dispatch (29997521777) FAILED at the node bake, and the cause was MPI-341's own new guard, not the 0.28 bump: PIP_CONSTRAINT pins torch by LOCAL version (2.12.0+cu130) which exists only on the pytorch index, but the constraint reaches EVERY pip call while --index-url was passed to exactly one. comfyui-impact-pack requires git+sam2, whose pyproject build-system.requires includes torch>=2.5.1, so pip builds it in a PEP-517 isolated build env that inherits the constraint but not the index -> ResolutionImpossible. Fixed in mpi-ci 16354e5 with ENV PIP_EXTRA_INDEX_URL for the cu130 index, which also closes the identical latent break at RUNTIME for the wrapper's connect-time node installs. Dockerfile.cpu bakes no torch, no twin. Re-dispatch 29998306717 green in 19m3s.

Step 5b boot smoke PASSES: the cpu dev tag serves /health 200 with wrapper_version 0.2.37 and 401s /wrapper/stats unauthenticated. MPI-341 check 3 also DONE, verified on the PULLED final cu130 image (sha256:80351c10) rather than build logs: /opt/constraints.txt holds the three +cu130 lines, pip list agrees, PIP_CONSTRAINT and PIP_EXTRA_INDEX_URL are both live in the image env (so the runtime guard covers the wrapper's connect-time installs), import torch reports 2.12.0+cu130 cuda 13.0, and /opt/ComfyUI/comfyui_version.py reports 0.28.0 - the core bump proven BAKED, not merely pinned. Build done-definition steps 1-4 satisfied; only the live Pod verify remains.

MPI-342 phase 4 code half: POD_IMAGE_VERSION_DEV and POD_IMAGE_VERSION_CPU_DEV moved v0.16.0 -> v0.17.0-dev in routes/remotePodLifecycle.js, the first time that pair has pointed anywhere other than the stable pin. Both dev tags are built, pushed, public and pull-verified, so the unbuilt-tag 404-at-boot hazard does not apply. Stable pins untouched at v0.16.0; node --check passes. STILL UNPROVEN: the live half - create a Pod from a source run and confirm it pulls the -dev tag while a released run still resolves v0.16.0. Needs an app restart first (these consts bake into the Express child at boot).

MPI-340 build-gated leg PROVEN on the cpu half (live, 2026-07-23, Pod 3ln3y4anycuort). A source run pulled ghcr.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.17.0-dev-cpu ('Downloaded newer image', digest sha256:3e1b6add) - the first time the dev IMAGE tag path has resolved anything other than the stable pin. Same boot also proved the dev RUNTIME channel: '[cubric-bootstrap] fetching runtime from https://pod.cubric.studio/vision/dev (channel=dev)', manifest channel=dev wrapper_version=0.2.37, wrapper.py and start-cpu.sh both installed from the dev prefix. Volume compat confirmed live: '[cubric] manifest stamped (schema 2, wrapper 0.2.37)' on a pre-existing volume holding 3 models - schema 2 matches the app's MANIFEST_SCHEMA_MAX so the gate passes, and the stamp merges rather than wipes. Download mode confirmed: 'ComfyManager disabled (download mode / no GPU)', /health 200. Left for the GPU half: same proof on v0.17.0-dev-cu130, plus the released-run-resolves-v0.16.0 side.

Loose end CLOSED: the app-side WRAPPER_VERSION = '0.2.36' pin in routes/remotePodLifecycle.js is INERT on any R2-floated boot. The Pod log shows '[cubric-bootstrap] unset CUBRIC_WRAPPER_VERSION - fetched wrapper self-reports version', so the bootstrap deliberately clears the app's env var and the fetched wrapper reports its own version (0.2.37 in the manifest stamp). No app-side bump needed, and no released-user risk from the lag.

MPI-340 build-gated leg now FULLY PROVEN - GPU half live 2026-07-23 (Pod 5sn0x7l1my2rvz). A source run pulled docker.io/madponyinteractive/cubric-vision-pod:v0.17.0-dev-cu130, 'Downloaded newer image', digest sha256:80351c10b7e7e991b5d5f426cc78041295ea8889335fda22200959ea93c8654c - byte-identical to what CI pushed and to the tag pull-verified locally, so the chain build->registry->RunPod is proven end to end on one digest. CUDA Version 13.0.3 confirms the cu130 base. Dev runtime channel fetched again (channel=dev, wrapper_version 0.2.37, wrapper.py from the dev prefix). Together with the cpu half earlier, BOTH legs of the dev IMAGE tag path have now resolved a real -dev tag rather than a tag equal to the stable pin - the branch MPI-340 could previously only ship half-proven. Still NOT build-gated and still open: the released-build-resolves-stable proof (fake a BUILD_HASH stamp) and one real promote run.
